name: Checkout and WLT payment-session handoff
problem: A client needs one server-owned checkout intent built from a valid cart and an owned serviceable address, while WLT remains the sole financial authority. Network ambiguity, retries and event replay must never create duplicate sessions, duplicate discounts or divergent projections.
goal: Create one tenant-scoped checkout intent, freeze operational pricing and address snapshots, create one idempotent WLT payment-session reference, project WLT outcomes atomically, and expose recoverable client and operator states.

excludedSurfaces.reason: The partner receives order truth in JRN-011, not pre-order checkout intent truth.
Captain execution begins after order and dispatch journeys.
Field operations do not own client checkout.
No active website checkout runtime is registered.

truthOwners.paymentProjectionInCheckout: DSH read-only projection from authenticated WLT events

transitions.trigger: authenticated WLT captured or cod_collected event
JRN-011 order creation where payment policy permits

negativeInvariants: A checkout intent cannot be created without an authenticated tenant and client.
A client cannot read or cancel another client's checkout intent.
Delivery checkout cannot proceed without an owned address and successful serviceability check.
One Idempotency-Key cannot represent two different checkout request fingerprints.
One checkout intent cannot attach two different WLT payment-session references.
DSH cannot capture, refund, settle or mutate a wallet balance.
A WLT event cannot cross tenant or payment-session boundaries.
A replayed event key cannot carry a different payload.
Checkout and coupon projections cannot commit partially for one WLT event.
Unknown WLT outcomes cannot cause creation of a second non-idempotent session.
No local or mocked checkout truth may replace server readback in a runtime screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Valid cart, address and serviceability create one priced checkout intent.
The WLT request uses dsh-checkout-intent:<intentId> as the stable idempotency key.
Ambiguous WLT outcomes remain recoverable by client polling and operator reconciliation.
Authenticated WLT events are recorded durably and applied atomically with coupon effects.
Duplicate identical events are safe and conflicting replays return a conflict.
Client UI renders loading, offline/error, unavailable, unknown, pending, failed, expired, cancelled and success paths.
Control panel exposes tenant, state, WLT reference, reconciliation age and governed filters without financial mutation controls.
Targeted static, Go, TypeScript, PostgreSQL runtime and DSH/WLT boundary checks pass on the same commit.
finalDecisionPolicy: Technical slices may reach READY_FOR_REVIEW after same-commit evidence. CLOSED_WITH_EVIDENCE additionally requires independent Product, QA/device/accessibility, Security/Finance and Release/Production approvals.
